title: macrozanonia philippinensis
intro: Macrozanonia philippinensis, also known as the Philippine stink ant tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the Philippines. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits include reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be made by drying the leaves, roots, and fruits, and then using them to create remedies for various ailments.
